html:
Exclusive to:

For sculptures players can repair using Suspicious Marble, see Marble Sculptures.
For decorative sculptures dropping Sketches when mined, see Marble Statues.

Chess Pieces are craftable objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. There are eight different variants that can be crafted at a Potter's Wheel and are mostly meant for decoration. Nearly all of them are unlocked by giving their respective Sketch to the Potter's Wheel, except for the Bubble Pipe Carving and the Carved Hornucopia, which are always available. Players are able to sculpt Chess Pieces out of two Rocks and one Marble, Cut Stone or Moon Shard. The chosen material determining the color of the crafted Chess Piece.

Chess Pieces can be carried by players in the chest slot, similar to Suspicious Marble, slowing down their movement speed and preventing them from several inventory actions, such as equipping a handheld item.

Contents

  • 1 Varg Figure
  • 2 Hound Figure
  • 3 Carrat Figure
  • 4 Beefalo Figure
  • 5 Kitcoon Figure
  • 6 Catcoon Figure
  • 7 Bunnyman Figure
  • 8 Start Tower Figure
  • 9 Gilded Depths Worm Figure
  • 10 Gilded Knight Figure
  • 11 Tips
  • 12 Trivia
  • 13 Gallery
  • 14 References

Varg Figure

Only during the Event: Year of the Varg

Varg Figures can be crafted from the Sculptures Filter after acquiring their respective Sketches from the Varg Shrine and giving them to a Potter's Wheel. Depending on whether the player chooses to place 1 Marble, 1 Cut Stone or 1 Moon Shard on the wheel before sculpting, the resulting Figure will be white, black or translucent green. Varg Figures additionally require 2 Rocks and 2 Hound's Teeth to craft, while the Hound Figures require 2 Rocks and 1 Hound's Tooth.

Similar to Chess Pieces, Varg and Hound Figures can be carried around on the players' backs and can be used for decorational purposes.

Back Model of Possessed Varg Figure:

  • Marble
  • Stone
  • Moonglass

Hound Figure

Only during the Event: Year of the Varg

Hound Figures can be crafted from the Sculptures Filter after acquiring their respective Sketches from the Varg Shrine and giving them to a Potter's Wheel. Depending on whether the player chooses to place 1 Marble, 1 Cut Stone or 1 Moon Shard on the wheel before sculpting, the resulting Figure will be white, black or translucent green. Varg Figures additionally require 2 Rocks and 2 Hound's Teeth to craft, while the Hound Figures require 2 Rocks and 1 Hound's Tooth.

Similar to Chess Pieces, Varg and Hound Figures can be carried around on the players' backs and can be used for decorational purposes.

Carrat Figure

Only during the Event: Year of the Carrat

The Carrat Figure can be crafted from the Sculptures Filter after acquiring its Sketch from the Carrat Shrine and giving it to a Potter's Wheel. Depending on whether the player chooses to place 1 Marble, 1 Cut Stone or 1 Moon Shard on the wheel before sculpting, the resulting Figure will be white, black or translucent green.

Similar to Chess Pieces, Carrat Figure can be carried around on the players' backs and can be used for decorational purposes.

Beefalo Figure

Only during the Event: Year of the Beefalo

The Beefalo Figure can be crafted from the Sculptures Filter after acquiring its Sketch from the Beefalo Shrine and giving it to a Potter's Wheel. Depending on whether the player chooses to place 1 Marble, 1 Cut Stone or 1 Moon Shard on the wheel before sculpting, the resulting Figure will be white, black or translucent green.

Similar to Chess Pieces, Beefalo Figure can be carried around on the players' backs and can be used for decorational purposes.

  • Stone
  • Moonglass

Tips

  • These structures act as impenetrable walls except to certain bosses. This can be used in the Moon Caller's Staff event to block off Spiders ,Werepigs and Hounds.

Trivia

  • Most Chess Pieces were added in the A Little Fixer Upper update, while the Giant Figures were introduced in an unnamed Bonus Update after the completion of A New Reign. The Bee Queen, Toadstool, Antlion, Ancient Fuelweaver, and Klaus figures were added in the May QoL update.
  • Based off of character quotes, it is implied that the Kingly Figure refers to Maxwell, the Queenly figure to Charlie, and the Pawn to all the other playable characters.
  • Maxwell's examination quote of the Pawn Figure lampshades the lack of a Clockwork Pawn enemy by stating that he doesn't know what a Pawn is.
  • The Queenly Figure used to have speech file lines referring to it having a "struggling" state, but these were later removed or commented out.
  • The Moon Shard figure type as well as the Moon Moth, Anchor, and "Moon" Figures were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
  • Knight, Bishop, and Rook Figures made from Moon Shards will not spawn Shadow Pieces if hammered during a new moon. This is intentional.[1]
  • In the Host of Horrors beta test, an early version of Armored Bearger Chess Pieces was discovered in the Nightmare Werepig Chess Pieces files.

Gallery

  • Chess Pieces as seen in the poster for the Arts and Crafts update.
  • Figure Concept Art from Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon
  • Figure Concept Art from Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon

References

  1. ↑ https://forums.kleientertainment.com/klei-bug-tracker/dont-starve-together-return-of-them/shadow-pieces-wont-spawn-upon-destroying-respective-chess-pieces-made-of-moon-shards-at-a-new-moon-night-r20548/

“That thing's the leashed of my worries.”
–Wilson, when examining a Varg Figure.
“It doesn't look so tough now.”
–Willow, when examining a Varg Figure.
“It looks like scary monster!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Varg Figure.
“It knows only fury.”
–Wendy, when examining a Varg Figure.
“THIS IS A TROPHY TO CELEBRATE MY GREATNESS”
–WX-78, when examining a Varg Figure.
“The sculptor clearly studied the creature closely.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Varg Figure.
“Not a wood sculpture, but it'll do.”
–Woodie, when examining a Varg Figure.
“What a gruesome maw.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Varg Figure.
“A testament tö a wörthy föe.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Varg Figure.
“That's a big, bad dog.”
–Webber, when examining a Varg Figure.
“I'm not sure why we memorialized this very scary creature.”
–Warly, when examining a Varg Figure.
“Stay stone, please”
–Wormwood, when examining a Varg Figure.
“It's a sculpture of that mean mutt.”
–Winona, when examining a Varg Figure.
“So scary, rendered still in stone.”
–Wortox, when examining a Varg Figure.
“Grrrr!”
–Wurt, when examining a Varg Figure.
“Poor Woby. She never seems to get along with the other dogs.”
–Walter, when examining a Varg Figure.
“I hope this doesn't come back to bite me.”
–Wanda, when examining a Varg Figure.

“Neigh machine?”
–Wormwood
“There were good and bad times with this fella.”
–Winona
“This little knight put up quite the fight.”
–Wortox
“Where spring now, springy Ironfolk?”
–Wurt
“It's a chess piece of a chess piece!”
–Walter
“I have mixed emotions about this one.”
–Wanda
Back Model of Gilded Knight Figure:
Marble
Stone
Moonglass
Tips[edit | edit source]
These structures act as impenetrable walls except to certain bosses. This can be used in the Moon Caller's Staff event to block off Spiders ,Werepigs and Hounds.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Most Chess Pieces were added in the A Little Fixer Upper update, while the Giant Figures were introduced in an unnamed Bonus Update after the completion of A New Reign. The Bee Queen, Toadstool, Antlion, Ancient Fuelweaver, and Klaus figures were added in the May QoL update.
Based off of character quotes, it is implied that the Kingly Figure refers to Maxwell, the Queenly figure to Charlie, and the Pawn to all the other playable characters.
Maxwell's examination quote of the Pawn Figure lampshades the lack of a Clockwork Pawn enemy by stating that he doesn't know what a Pawn is.
The Queenly Figure used to have speech file lines referring to it having a "struggling" state, but these were later removed or commented out.
The Moon Shard figure type as well as the Moon Moth, Anchor, and "Moon" Figures were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
Knight, Bishop, and Rook Figures made from Moon Shards will not spawn Shadow Pieces if hammered during a new moon. This is intentional.[1]
In the Host of Horrors beta test, an early version of Armored Bearger Chess Pieces was discovered in the Nightmare Werepig Chess Pieces files.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
Chess Pieces as seen in the poster for the Arts and Crafts update.
Figure Concept Art from Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon
Figure Concept Art from Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Quân Cờ là vật phẩm có thể chế tạo độc quyền trong Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong A New Reign. Có tám biến thể khác nhau có thể được chế tạo tại Bàn thợ gốm và chủ yếu dùng để trang trí. Gần như tất cả chúng đều được mở khóa bằng cách đưa Bản phác thảo tương ứng của chúng cho Bàn thợ gốm, ngoại trừ Khắc ống bong bóng và Khắc sừng, luôn có sẵn. Người chơi có thể điêu khắc các Quân cờ từ hai Đá và một Cẩm thạch, Đá cắt hoặc Mảnh trăng. Vật liệu được chọn quyết định màu sắc của Quân cờ được chế tạo.
title: Chess Pieces/Event
wikitext:
{{Navbutton|head}} {{Navbutton|link=Chess Pieces|text=Common}} {{Navbutton|link=Chess Pieces/Bosses|text=Bosses}} {{Navbutton|link=Chess Pieces/Event|text=Event}} {{Navbutton|end}} {{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{For|sculptures players can repair using [[Suspicious Marble]]|Marble Sculptures}} {{For|decorative sculptures dropping [[Sketch]]es when mined|Marble Statues}} '''Chess Pieces''' are craftable objects exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[A New Reign]]''. There are eight different variants that can be crafted at a [[Potter's Wheel]] and are mostly meant for decoration. Nearly all of them are unlocked by giving their respective [[Sketch]] to the Potter's Wheel, except for the Bubble Pipe Carving and the Carved Hornucopia, which are always available. Players are able to sculpt Chess Pieces out of two [[Rocks]] and one [[Marble]], [[Cut Stone]] or [[ Moon Shard]]. The chosen material determining the color of the crafted Chess Piece. Chess Pieces can be carried by players in the chest slot, similar to [[Suspicious Marble]], slowing down their movement speed and preventing them from several inventory actions, such as equipping a handheld item. ==Varg Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |name = Varg Figure |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Varg Figure.png|Marble Varg Figure (Stone).png|Stone Varg Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |image = Varg Figure Marble Build.png|Marble Varg Figure Stone Build.png|Stone Varg Figure Moonglass Build.png|Moonglass |description = Sculpt a fearsome Clay Varg. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_claywarg_marble"
"chesspiece_claywarg_stone"
"chesspiece_claywarg_moonglass"}} {{Event|Year of the Varg}} {{Quotes |wilson = That thing's the leashed of my worries. |willow = It doesn't look so tough now. |wolfgang = It looks like scary monster! |wendy = It knows only fury. |wx78 = THIS IS A TROPHY TO CELEBRATE MY GREATNESS |wickerbottom = The sculptor clearly studied the creature closely. |woodie = Not a wood sculpture, but it'll do. |maxwell = What a gruesome maw. |wigfrid = A testament tö a wörthy föe. |webber = That's a big, bad dog. |winona = It's a sculpture of that mean mutt. |warly = I'm not sure why we memorialized this very scary creature. |wortox = So scary, rendered still in stone. |wormwood = Stay stone, please |wurt = Grrrr! |walter = Poor Woby. She never seems to get along with the other dogs. |wanda = I hope this doesn't come back to bite me. |choose = webber |source = when examining a Varg Figure.}} '''Varg Figures''' can be crafted from the [[Sculptures Filter]] after acquiring their respective [[Year of the Varg#Figure Sketches|Sketches]] from the Varg Shrine and giving them to a [[Potter's Wheel]]. Depending on whether the player chooses to place 1 [[Marble]], 1 [[Cut Stone]] or 1 [[Moon Shard]] on the wheel before sculpting, the resulting Figure will be white, black or translucent green. Varg Figures additionally require 2 [[Rocks]] and 2 [[Hound's Tooth|Hound's Teeth]] to craft, while the Hound Figures require 2 Rocks and 1 Hound's Tooth. Similar to [[Chess Pieces]], Varg and Hound Figures can be carried around on the players' backs and can be used for decorational purposes. Back Model of Possessed Varg Figure:
File:Statue Varg Marble Back.png|Marble File:Statue Varg Stone Back.png|Stone File:Statue Varg Moonglass Back.png|Moonglass
{{Clear}} ==Hound Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |name = Hound Figure |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Hound Figure.png|Marble Hound Figure (Stone).png|Stone Hound Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |image = Hound Figure Marble Build.png|Marble Hound Figure Stone Build.png|Stone Hound Figure Moonglass Build.png|Moonglass |description = It ain't nothing but a clay hound dog. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_clayhound_marble"
"chesspiece_clayhound_stone"
"chesspiece_clayhound_moonglass"}} {{Event|Year of the Varg}} {{Quotes |wilson = And I didn't even get eaten! |willow = It almost looks friendly. |wolfgang = Is nice little doggy. |wendy = Happiness is a warm puppy... but this one is cold. |wx78 = A GOOD ORGANIC SPECIES. IT OBEYS ITS MASTER |wickerbottom = A replica of Canis lupis. |woodie = All the dog, none of the smell. |maxwell = It's quite fetching. |wigfrid = A höund öf the hunt! |webber = Puppy! |winona = A good boy if ever I saw one. |warly = I'm glad it's not trying to bite me. |wortox = A clay hound made from that sketch we found! |wormwood = Alive? Hmmm... |wurt = Bad doggy. |walter = He wasn't a very good dog. |wanda = Well, it hasn't sprung to life yet. So far so good. |choose = woodie |source = when examining a Hound Figure.}} '''Hound Figures''' can be crafted from the [[Sculptures Filter]] after acquiring their respective [[Year of the Varg#Figure Sketches|Sketches]] from the Varg Shrine and giving them to a [[Potter's Wheel]]. Depending on whether the player chooses to place 1 [[Marble]], 1 [[Cut Stone]] or 1 [[Moon Shard]] on the wheel before sculpting, the resulting Figure will be white, black or translucent green. Varg Figures additionally require 2 [[Rocks]] and 2 [[Hound's Tooth|Hound's Teeth]] to craft, while the Hound Figures require 2 Rocks and 1 Hound's Tooth. Similar to [[Chess Pieces]], Varg and Hound Figures can be carried around on the players' backs and can be used for decorational purposes. {{Clear}} ==Carrat Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |name = Carrat Figure |icon= Carrat Figure.png|Marble Carrat Figure (Stone).png|Stone Carrat Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |image = Carrat Figure Marble Build.png|Marble Carrat Figure Stone Build.png|Stone Carrat Figure Moonglass Build.png|Moonglass |description = A winsome sculpture of a champion. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_carrat_marble"
"chesspiece_carrat_stone"
"chesspiece_carrat_moonglass"}}{{Event|Year of the Carrat}} {{Quotes |wilson = We have a winner! |willow = Now all I can think of is roasted carrots. |wolfgang = Wolfgang liked racing leggy carrots. |wendy = If only I could feel so serene. |wx78 = WHEN WILL ONE OF MY UNDERLINGS CARVE A STATUE OF ME? |wickerbottom = A delightfully rendered likeness of the Daucus carota rattus. |woodie = Looks well made. |maxwell = If only I had a mantle to set it on. |wigfrid = I'm nöt keen ön hönöring a vegetable sö. |webber = It makes us feel content. |winona = Look at it, just lazing around! |warly = Looking at it is making me a little hungry... |wortox = Not so light on its feet now, hyuyu! |wormwood = Friends! |wurt = Veggie rat look happy. |walter = I usually prefer badges to trophies, but I might make an exception. |wanda = I don't think this really warranted a statue. |choose = wx78}} The '''Carrat Figure''' can be crafted from the [[Sculptures Filter]] after acquiring its [[Year of the Carrat#Carrat Figure Sketch|Sketch]] from the Carrat Shrine and giving it to a [[Potter's Wheel]]. Depending on whether the player chooses to place 1 [[Marble]], 1 [[Cut Stone]] or 1 [[Moon Shard]] on the wheel before sculpting, the resulting Figure will be white, black or translucent green. Similar to [[Chess Pieces]], Carrat Figure can be carried around on the players' backs and can be used for decorational purposes. {{Clear}} ==Beefalo Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |name = Beefalo Figure |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Beefalo Figure.png|Marble Beefalo Figure (Stone).png|Stone Beefalo Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |image = Beefalo Figure Marble Build.png|Marble Beefalo Figure Stone Build.png|Stone Beefalo Figure Moonglass Build.png|Moonglass |description = A winsome sculpture of a champion. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_beefalo_marble"
"chesspiece_beefalo_stone"
"chesspiece_beefalo_moonglass"}}{{Event|Year of the Beefalo}} {{Quotes |wilson = This sculpture is pretty beefy. |willow = Hey, they got his good side! |wolfgang = Is look almost like real hair-cow. |wendy = Merely a cold, lifeless copy. |wx78 = AN IMPROVEMENT ON THE FLESHY ORIGINAL |wickerbottom = It captures my beefalo's likeness quite well. |woodie = Some real fine detail on the fur there. |maxwell = At least this version doesn't shed all over my poor suit. |wigfrid = A stöne tribute tö my faithful steed. |webber = Now we want to go for a beefalo ride! |warly = What a beautifully marbled steak. |wormwood = Not fluffy... |winona = Aw, it looks just like my beefalo. |wortox = This beefalo is even more hard-headed than most! |wurt = It just rock, real thing better. |walter = Aw don't be jealous Woby, you deserve a statue too! |wanda = A real one would be marginally more interesting to look at. |choose = wilson |source = when examining a Beefalo Figure }} The '''Beefalo Figure''' can be crafted from the [[Sculptures Filter]] after acquiring its [[Year of the Beefalo#Beefalo Figure Sketch|Sketch]] from the Beefalo Shrine and giving it to a [[Potter's Wheel]]. Depending on whether the player chooses to place 1 [[Marble]], 1 [[Cut Stone]] or 1 [[Moon Shard]] on the wheel before sculpting, the resulting Figure will be white, black or translucent green. Similar to [[Chess Pieces]], Beefalo Figure can be carried around on the players' backs and can be used for decorational purposes. {{Clear}} ==Kitcoon Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |name = Kitcoon Figure |icon= Kitcoon Figure.png|Marble Kitcoon Figure (Stone).png|Stone Kitcoon Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |image = Kitcoon Figure Marble Build.png|Marble Kitcoon Figure Stone Build.png|Stone Kitcoon Figure Moonglass Build.png|Moonglass |description = One's true wealth is measured by their friends. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |tier = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_kitcoon_marble"
"chesspiece_kitcoon_stone"
"chesspiece_kitcoon_moonglass" |filter={{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}}}} {{Event|Year of the Catcoon}} {{Quotes |wilson = These ones are much easier to find. |willow = I wanna push it over. |wolfgang = Wolfgang can lift twice as many! |wendy = Hubris, undoubtedly. |wx78 = THE URGE TO TOPPLE IT OVERWHELMS MY SYSTEMS |wickerbottom = I should warn the children not to play too close to it. |woodie = Usually somethin' like this would be carved outta wood. |maxwell = This is a rather precarious design. |wigfrid = A mighty pillar för a mighty family! |webber = Whoah! I hope they don't fall over... |winona = I can't tell if this a great feat of engineering or a very stupid one. Either way, it sure is cute. |warly = I think Mme. Wickerbottom is particularly fond of this one. |wortox = They'll reach the sky on high! |wormwood = Not hiding? |wurt = Aww, they having a nap. |walter = They're pretty cute, but not as cute as you Woby. |wanda = Oh, a precariously tall statue, what could go wrong? |choose = winona |source = examining a Kitcoon Figure}} {{Clear}} ==Catcoon Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |name = Catcoon Figure |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Catcoon Figure.png|Marble Catcoon Figure (Stone).png|Stone Catcoon Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |image = Catcoon Figure Marble Build.png|Marble Catcoon Figure Stone Build.png|Stone Catcoon Figure Moonglass Build.png|Moonglass |description = A curious statue for a curious creature. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |tier = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_catcoon_marble"
"chesspiece_catcoon_stone"
"chesspiece_catcoon_moonglass" |filter={{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}}}} {{Event|Year of the Catcoon}} {{Quotes |wilson = It would probably make a great scratching post. |willow = Heh. I like this one. |wolfgang = Ha! Is easy to find this one. |wendy = A reminder of brighter days. |wx78 = A CLEAR IMPROVEMENT OVER ITS FLESHY COUNTERPART |wickerbottom = The resemblance to the Felis lybica is incredible. |woodie = Real nice detailing on the fur there. |maxwell = This animal commands respect through violence. |wigfrid = Yöu have my respect, fellöw hunter. |webber = We respect the butterfly hunter. |winona = Aw, whatcha got there? ...Oh. |warly = At least this one won't try to sneak off with my lunch. |wortox = This little guy and I don't see eye to eye, hyuyu. |wormwood = No tail swish swoosh |wurt = It pretty good statue, has dead butterfly! |walter = It's not quite as exciting as a monster statue, is it? |wanda = I guess I did have time to play with you after all. |choose = warly |source = examining a Catcoon Figure}} {{Clear}} == Bunnyman Figure == {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |name = Bunnyman Figure |icon= Bunnyman Figure.png|Marble Bunnyman Figure (Stone).png|Stone Bunnyman Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |description = A fluffy friend, made of rock. |image = Bunnyman Figure Marble Build.png|Marble Bunnyman Figure Stone Build.png|Stone Bunnyman Figure Moonglass Build.png|Moonglass |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |tier_text = {{Pic32|Bunnyman Figure Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_manrabbit_marble"
"chesspiece_manrabbit_stone"
"chesspiece_manrabbit_moonglass" |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}}}} {{Event|Year of the Bunnyman}} {{Quotes |wilson = I want to hug it, but the stone chafes. |willow = Good times. |wolfgang = Haha! Funny rabbit thought it could beat Wolfgang! |wendy = A fleeting moment of joy, captured in stone. |wx78 = THEY'RE SO MUCH BETTER WHEN THEY'RE NOT MOVING |wickerbottom = Those lagomorphs are quite the rascals. |woodie = Someone worked hard on that. |maxwell = Who put this ridiculous thing here? |wigfrid = Their weapöns were strange, but they pössessed a warriör's spirit. |webber = It's our most huggable statue. |warly = As far as battles go, it was probably the most pleasant. |wormwood = Big Hopper |winona = It doesn't look like he has a care in the world. |wortox = Looking at it fills me with hoppiness, hyuyu! |wurt = Glurgh. Bunnyman get statue before Mermfolk?! |walter = He looks so happy! And hoppy! |wanda = I suppose it wasn't the most unpleasant diversion. |choose = wilson |source = when examining a Bunnyman Figure }} {{Clear}} == Start Tower Figure == {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |name = Start Tower Figure |icon= Start Tower Figure.png|Marble Start Tower Figure (Stone).png|Stone Start Tower Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |description = A tribute to getting things off to a fiery start. |image = Start Tower Figure Marble Dropped.png|Marble Start Tower Figure Stone Dropped.png|Stone Start Tower Figure Moonglass Dropped.png|Moonglass |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |tier_text = {{Pic32|Start Tower Figure Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_yotd_marble"
"chesspiece_yotd_stone"
"chesspiece_yotd_moonglass" |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}}}} {{Event|Year of the Dragonfly}} {{Quotes |wilson = Just the sight of it gets my heart racing! |willow = Too many boats, not enough fire. |wolfgang = Wolfgang crushed Dragonfly race! |wendy = If only I could have a new start. |wx78 = I HATE BOAT RACES |wickerbottom = A curious combination; the fire-breathing Diptera and seafaring. |woodie = Some nice stonework. Would've looked even better carved outta pine. |maxwell = Always nice to be reminded of my victories. |wigfrid = A mönument tö my triumph. |webber = We had a lot of fun that day! |warly = Ah, the sweet taste of victory! |wormwood = Fire! Oh... is rock |winona = We really brought the heat! Ha! |wortox = From hot to stone cold. |wurt = Why it not start? Wanna race again! |walter = Racing was fun, even if I didn't get a badge for it... |wanda = Back where it all began... the boat race, anyway. |choose = wilson |source = when examining a Start Tower Figure }} Back Model of Start Tower Figure:
Start Tower Figure Marble Back.png|Marble Start Tower Figure Stone Back.png|Stone Start Tower Figure Moonglass Back.png|Moonglass
{{Clear}} == Gilded Depths Worm Figure == {{Object Infobox |equipslot = body |name = Gilded Depths Worm Figure |icon= Gilded Depths Worm Figure.png|Marble Gilded Depths Worm Figure (Stone).png|Stone Gilded Depths Worm Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |description = A reminder of a lucky worm encounter. |image = Gilded Depths Worm Figure Marble Dropped.png|Marble Gilded Depths Worm Figure Stone Dropped.png|Stone Gilded Depths Worm Figure Moonglass Dropped.png|Moonglass |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |tier_text = {{Pic32|Gilded Depths Worm Figure Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_yots_marble"
"chesspiece_yots_stone"
"chesspiece_yots_moonglass" |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}}}} {{Event|Year of the Depths Worm}} {{Quotes |wilson = I usually try to stay away from gold diggers. |willow = Still gross. |wolfgang = Worm was no match for Wolfgang. |wendy = How curious, the things we choose to commemorate. |wx78 = PATHETIC FLESH LOG |wickerbottom = One of the hungriest large invertebrates. |woodie = It would've looked better carved out of wood. |maxwell = Let's leave this underground. |wigfrid = I dö relish thy slaying, wörm. |webber = We can play with this worm! |winona = Ugh, still makes my skin crawl. |warly = I remember the chewing. |wortox = Make no error facing this writhing terror. |wormwood = Little wiggly |wurt = Cute little worm. |walter = The carving still looks slithery! |wanda = And I just managed to forget you. |choose = wilson }} Back Model of Gilded Depths Worm Figure:
Gilded Depths Worm Figure Marble Back.png|Marble Gilded Depths Worm Figure Stone Back.png|Stone Gilded Depths Worm Figure Moonglass Back.png|Moonglass
{{Clear}} == Gilded Knight Figure == {{Object Infobox |equipslot = body |name = Gilded Knight Figure |icon= File:Gilded Knight Figure.png|Marble Gilded Knight Figure (Stone).png|Stone Gilded Knight Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |description = Shining armor included. |image = Gilded Knight Figure Marble Dropped.png|Marble Gilded Knight Figure Stone Dropped.png|Stone Gilded Knight Figure Moonglass Dropped.png|Moonglass |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |tier_text = {{Pic32|Gilded Knight Figure Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_yoth_marble"

{{Clear}} =={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * These structures act as impenetrable walls except to certain bosses. This can be used in the Moon Caller's Staff event to block off [[Spider|Spiders]] ,[[Werepigs]] and [[Hounds]]. =={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Most Chess Pieces were added in the ''[[A New Reign#September 1st, 2016 - A Little Fixer Upper|A Little Fixer Upper]]'' update, while the Giant Figures were introduced in an unnamed [[Don't Starve Together/Version History#May 18th, 2017 - Bonus Update|Bonus Update]] after the completion of ''A New Reign''. The Bee Queen, Toadstool, Antlion, Ancient Fuelweaver, and Klaus figures were added in the [[Don't Starve Together/Version History|May]] QoL update. * Based off of character quotes, it is implied that the Kingly Figure refers to Maxwell, the Queenly figure to Charlie, and the Pawn to all the other playable characters. *Maxwell's examination quote of the Pawn Figure lampshades the lack of a Clockwork Pawn enemy by stating that he doesn't know what a Pawn is. * The Queenly Figure used to have speech file lines referring to it having a "struggling" state, but these were later removed or commented out. * The Moon Shard figure type as well as the Moon Moth, Anchor, and "Moon" Figures were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Turn of Tides|Turn of Tides]]'' update. * Knight, Bishop, and Rook Figures made from Moon Shards will not spawn Shadow Pieces if hammered during a new moon. This is intentional.https://forums.kleientertainment.com/klei-bug-tracker/dont-starve-together-return-of-them/shadow-pieces-wont-spawn-upon-destroying-respective-chess-pieces-made-of-moon-shards-at-a-new-moon-night-r20548/ * In the Host of Horrors beta test, an early version of Armored Bearger Chess Pieces was discovered in the Nightmare Werepig Chess Pieces files. == {{pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == DSTupdate artsandcrafts.png|Chess Pieces as seen in the poster for the ''[[A New Reign#October 13, 2016 - Arts and Crafts|Arts and Crafts]]'' update.
